text:
There once was a little old owl who lived quietly in her tree. Every morning she’d flap her wings softly and greet the sun with a sleepy hoot. Life was easy for her — she didn’t have worries, no demands to meet, just lots of quiet time. But one day a sly fox came swooping down.

“Who goes there?” the owl crooned suspiciously.

“A visitor,” said the fox. “Quite lonely?”

“I’m not bored!”

“Hmm.” The fox snickered.

The owl watched him. She didn’t trust mere words. But nothing nefarious seemed forthcoming. Just curiosity.

“Well,” said the fox with a grin, “I’ll tell you a secret. Ever had possum milk?”

“A what now?”

“A possum!”

A pause.

“Oh…”

The fox laughed. “You’ve got big ears!”

And that evening, with an owl feathered hat collecting twigs, they drank milk. It was strange, but perhaps there was something
